entries.question: What is Verlin Labs?
entries.answer: Verlin Labs — Understand complex AI concepts through clear mental models. Free 2-hour sessions, hands-on programs for students, engineers, and product managers.

entries.question: How do I book the free session?
entries.answer: Visit the Free Session page, pick a time slot on the calendar, and confirm your booking. You'll get an immediate confirmation and calendar invite by email. No credit card required.

entries.question: What are the course prices?
entries.answer: Current introductory pricing: Students: ₹2,999 introductory (list ₹9,999, 8 days · 2 phases). Engineers: ₹3,999 introductory (list ₹14,999, 10 days · 3 phases). Product Managers: ₹6,999 introductory (list ₹24,999, 16 days · 4 phases). The 2-hour intro session is always free.

entries.question: What is the introductory pricing offer?
entries.answer: All full programs currently have an introductory discount off list price. School Students: ₹2,999 (was ₹9,999). College Engineers: ₹3,999 (was ₹14,999). Product Managers: ₹6,999 (was ₹24,999). The free 2-hour session remains completely free.

entries.question: What is a mental model?
entries.answer: A mental model is a simple framework that helps you understand how something works. Instead of memorizing facts, you learn the structure behind AI systems — so new topics feel familiar instead of overwhelming.

entries.question: What does "clarity-first learning" mean?
entries.answer: We start with frameworks that make complex ideas feel structured and predictable. Instead of dumping information, we build understanding step by step — so you can explain concepts in your own words and apply them to new situations.
bullets: Visual mental models before jargon
Live explanation and Q&A, not passive video dumps
Audience-tailored examples for your background

entries.question: Is this suitable for complete beginners?
entries.answer: Yes. The free session and student track are designed for first exposure to AI. We use plain language and visual frameworks. Engineers and professionals get more depth, but we never assume you already know the topic.

entries.question: Is the 2-hour session really free?
entries.answer: Yes — the full introductory session is free. No enrollment fee, no credit card to book, and no hidden charges during or after the session.
bullets: No payment information collected at booking
You are never auto-enrolled in a paid program
Paid enrollment is optional and only discussed if it fits your goals

entries.question: Will there be any sales pitch during the session?
entries.answer: No hard sell. The session is a structured teaching experience — mental models, a live AI walkthrough, hands-on exercise, and Q&A. We may mention the full program if it's relevant to your goals, but there's no pressure or time-limited tactics.

entries.question: What happens after the free session?
entries.answer: You'll receive a follow-up email within 1–2 business days with a summary, key frameworks, and suggested next steps for your track.
bullets: Curated starter resource pack
Optional overview of the full Verlin Labs program
No obligation to enroll

entries.question: How soon can I get a slot?
entries.answer: Availability depends on the calendar — weekdays typically have more open times than weekends. Book through the Free Session page and you'll see real-time slots. Confirmation is immediate, with a calendar invite by email.

entries.question: What's the difference between the three tracks?
entries.answer: All tracks share the same clarity-first framework, but examples, pace, and project depth adapt to your background.
bullets: School Students (Classes 6–12) — friendly analogies, safe AI tool use, showcase project (8-day program)
College Engineers — LLM fundamentals to portfolio projects and interview prep (10-day intensive)
Product Managers — AI-first PM workflow, discovery, PRDs, MVP build, capstone demo (16-day program)

entries.question: How long are the programs?
entries.answer: The student track runs 8 days, the engineer track 10 days, and the product manager track 16 days. Each includes live sessions, guided exercises, and project work. Exact schedules are shared during enrollment.

entries.question: Do I get a certificate?
entries.answer: Completing the full program includes a Verlin Labs completion certificate and capstone presentation. The free introductory session does not include a certificate — it's an experience session to help you decide if the full track fits.

entries.question: Can I switch tracks later?
entries.answer: If you realize another track is a better fit, contact us before the program advances too far. We'll help you move when pacing and content overlap allow. Starting with the free session is the best way to confirm your track before enrolling.

entries.question: How is this different from YouTube or other online courses?
entries.answer: Most content optimizes for watch time. Verlin Labs optimizes for understanding — live mental models, mentor Q&A, audience-tailored pacing, and hands-on exercises with feedback. You leave knowing what to do next, not just what to watch next.

entries.question: Do I need any technical background?
entries.answer: Not for the free session or student track. Engineer and PM tracks go deeper, but we still build from frameworks first. Bring curiosity; we handle structure and scaffolding.

entries.question: What tools will I use?
entries.answer: Tools vary by track — from accessible AI assistants and no-code builders for students and PMs, to coding environments and APIs for engineers. We introduce tools only after the underlying concept is clear, and we never require expensive paid software for the free session.

entries.question: Are the sessions live or recorded?
entries.answer: The free session and core program sessions are live and interactive. Enrolled learners in the full program get access to session recordings and structured modules through the My Course dashboard for review.

entries.question: Do you offer corporate or team training?
entries.answer: Yes. We run clarity-first AI literacy workshops for teams — tailored examples, live Q&A, and follow-up resources. Contact us with your team size, goals, and timeline for options.

entries.question: Can we customize a program for our team?
entries.answer: Yes. We can adapt modules, case studies, and pacing for your industry and skill mix. Share your context through the contact form and we'll propose a structure that fits.

entries.question: What time zones do you support?
entries.answer: Sessions are scheduled in India Standard Time (IST) by default, with slots designed for learners in India and compatible regions. If you're in another time zone, mention it when booking or contacting us — we'll suggest the best available options.

entries.question: How are the sessions conducted?
entries.answer: Online via video conferencing. You'll receive a link after booking. A laptop or tablet with stable internet is recommended. Camera and microphone are optional but helpful for interaction.

entries.question: What if I miss a session?
entries.answer: For the free session, reschedule via your confirmation email (24 hours' notice when possible). Enrolled program learners can review recordings in My Course and contact support for catch-up guidance on missed live days.

entries.question: Is the free session really free?
entries.answer: Yes — completely free with no hidden fees. We want you to experience our teaching style before considering the full course. No credit card is required to book.

entries.question: Do I need any prior AI knowledge?
entries.answer: No. We start from clarity-first foundations and adapt depth to your track — students, engineers, or product managers. Bring curiosity; we handle the structure.

entries.question: How is this different from YouTube or courses?
entries.answer: Most content dumps information. Verlin Labs teaches through live mental models, audience-tailored pacing, hands-on exercises, and mentor Q&A — so understanding sticks and you know what to do next.

entries.question: Can I join if I'm a complete beginner?
entries.answer: Absolutely. The student track and free session are designed for first exposure to AI. We use plain language, visual frameworks, and no jargon until the idea itself is clear.

entries.question: Who is this for?
entries.answer: School students (Classes 6–12), college engineers, and product managers. When you book, you select your track so examples and pace match your background.

entries.question: Can I reschedule my session?
entries.answer: Yes. Use the link in your confirmation email to reschedule up to 24 hours before your session.

entries.question: Is the introductory session really free?
entries.answer: Yes — the full 2-hour introductory session is completely free. There is no enrollment fee, no credit card required to book, and no hidden charges during or after the session. We offer this session so you can experience how Verlin Labs teaches — through clarity, mental models, and live interaction — before making any decision about a paid program. Many learning platforms use a short sales call; we use a structured teaching session instead.
bullets: No payment information is collected when you book
You are never auto-enrolled in a paid program
Enrollment in the full track is optional and only discussed if it fits your goals

entries.question: Who is this session designed for?
entries.answer: The session is designed for learners at different stages who want to understand AI and technology with clarity — not hype. When you book, you select the audience track that best matches your background so examples, pace, and exercises feel relevant to you. Each track covers the same clarity-first framework, but the scenarios and depth are adapted for your context.
bullets: School students (Classes 6–12) — intuitive explanations, relatable examples, confidence-building pace
College engineers — structured frameworks, technical depth, bridge from theory to application
Working professionals & product managers — decision-ready mental models, product and strategy context
Parents or educators booking for a student are welcome — note the learner's level when booking

entries.question: How long is the session?
entries.answer: The session is scheduled for 2 hours in total. We follow a published agenda with timed segments so you always know what is coming next and how long each part will take. A short comfort break is built into the flow. If you need to step away briefly, you can rejoin without missing the overall structure — we recap key transitions at each segment.
bullets: 0:00–0:15 — Welcome and goal alignment
0:15–0:45 — Mental models primer (live demonstration)
0:45–1:15 — Audience-tailored AI deep-dive
1:15–1:45 — Guided hands-on exercise
1:45–2:00 — Personalized learning path and open Q&A

entries.question: What should I prepare beforehand?
entries.answer: Very little preparation is required — the session is designed to be accessible on the day you join. Most attendees spend fewer than 10 minutes getting ready. We recommend joining from a quiet space with a stable internet connection so you can participate in discussion and the hands-on segment comfortably.
bullets: Laptop or tablet with camera/microphone (either is optional but helpful for interaction)
Stable internet connection — wired or strong Wi‑Fi is ideal for screen sharing moments
Notebook or digital notes if you like capturing frameworks (not required)
One question you would like clarity on — AI, learning, or your career path
No pre-reading, coding environment, or paid tools required

entries.question: Do I need a technical or AI background?
entries.answer: No prior AI or programming background is required. The session is intentionally clarity-first: we start with mental models and build understanding step by step rather than assuming specialist knowledge. If you do have a technical background, you will still benefit — we go deeper on application and trade-offs in the engineer and professional tracks. If you are completely new, you will not be left behind; jargon is introduced only after the underlying idea is clear.
bullets: Beginners receive plain-language explanations and visual frameworks
Engineers explore how concepts connect to systems, data, and implementation choices
Professionals focus on product decisions, communication, and stakeholder context
All tracks include live Q&A — ask at whatever level makes sense for you

entries.question: Can I reschedule or cancel?
entries.answer: Yes — you can reschedule or cancel from the link in your confirmation email. We ask for at least 24 hours' notice so the slot can be offered to another learner on the waitlist. If you need to change plans inside the 24-hour window due to an emergency, contact us and we will do our best to accommodate you. Late cancellations may be limited to one free change per booking.
bullets: Reschedule or cancel via the confirmation email link (no login required)
Free changes up to 24 hours before your scheduled start time
You will receive an updated calendar invite after rescheduling
No-show without notice may require rebooking through the contact page

entries.question: What happens after the session ends?
entries.answer: After the session, you receive a concise follow-up by email within 1–2 business days. This summarizes what we covered, highlights the frameworks we used, and outlines sensible next steps based on your audience track and goals. If the full program is a good fit, we share enrollment information and syllabus details — but there is no hard sell and no time-limited pressure. Many attendees use only the free session and resources to continue learning independently.
bullets: Session summary and key mental models recap
Personalized learning path recommendation for your track
Optional overview of the full Verlin Labs program structure
Invitation to book office hours or contact support if you have follow-up questions
No obligation to enroll — you decide if and when to continue

entries.question: Will I receive resources afterward?
entries.answer: Yes. Every attendee receives a curated starter pack of resources selected for their audience track. These are meant to help you keep momentum after the live session, not replace it. Resources typically include reading links, framework cheat-sheets, and suggested practice prompts. Full program learners later get access to session recordings, structured modules, and progress tracking through the My Course dashboard.
bullets: Starter reading list and framework reference sheet (PDF or Notion-style summary)
Practice prompts based on the hands-on segment from your session
Links to relevant Verlin Labs library and mental model guides
Live session recordings are not shared by default to protect open discussion
Enrolled program learners access recordings and syllabus via My Course

entries.question: How quickly will I get a reply?
entries.answer: We aim to respond within 24–48 hours on business days. Urgent free-session scheduling questions are usually faster — often the same day.

entries.question: Can I book a session directly?
entries.answer: Yes. For the fastest path to a free 2-hour session, use our booking page. The contact form is best for custom questions, teams, or partnerships.

entries.question: Is there a physical office I can visit?
entries.answer: Verlin Labs programs are delivered online. Sessions use video conferencing so learners can join from anywhere with a stable connection.

entries.question: Will you try to sell me a paid program?
entries.answer: No pressure. We'll answer your question honestly. Paid enrollment is discussed only if it's genuinely relevant to your goals — many people use only the free session and library resources.

entries.question: What should I include in my message?
entries.answer: Your background, what you're trying to learn or solve, team size (if applicable), and any timeline. The more context you share, the more useful our reply will be.

entries.question: Who is the School Students track for?
entries.answer: Make AI and tech click — without the jargon. An 8-day friendly program with fun analogies, safe AI tool use, and a hands-on showcase project.

entries.question: Who is the College Engineers track for?
entries.answer: Go beyond tutorials — build real understanding. A 10-day intensive from LLM fundamentals to portfolio projects and interview prep.

entries.question: Who is the Product Managers track for?
entries.answer: Master AI-powered product management — from discovery to MVP launch. A 16-day program built for PMs who want to lead with AI tools, ship real products, and drive growth with confidence.

entries.question: Tell me about the AI Explorers for School Students program
entries.answer: An 8-day friendly program that helps school students understand AI, apps, and the digital world through fun analogies, safe tool use, and hands-on mini projects. Duration: 8 days · 2 phases. Introductory price: ₹2,999 (list ₹9,999, save ₹7,000).

entries.question: Tell me about the AI Engineering Foundations program
entries.answer: A 10-day intensive program for engineering students who want deep intuition for AI systems, hands-on API projects, and portfolio-ready work for internships and interviews. Duration: 10 days · 3 phases. Introductory price: ₹3,999 (list ₹14,999, save ₹11,000).

entries.question: Tell me about the AI Foundations for Product Managers program
entries.answer: A 16-day intensive program designed for Product Managers who want to master AI tools, lead product discovery, build MVPs with vibe coding, and launch growth-ready products. Duration: 16 days · 4 phases. Introductory price: ₹6,999 (list ₹24,999, save ₹18,000).

entries.question: Where can I read articles and guides?
entries.answer: The Library has clarity-first articles on AI fundamentals, LLMs, transformers, and more. Mental Models covers reusable frameworks. The blog shares weekly insights.

entries.question: How do I subscribe to the newsletter?
entries.answer: Weekly mental models, frameworks, and clarity-first insights — no noise.

entries.question: How do I contact Verlin Labs?
entries.answer: Use the contact form for custom questions, team training, or partnerships. For booking a free session, use the Free Session page directly for fastest scheduling.
